title: Regime-specific sigmoid stacking instead of isotonic calibration
summary: For ROC AUC, preserving rank matters.
rationale: For ROC AUC, preserving rank matters. Scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change AUC because it introduces ties, while sigmoid is strictly monotonic. Since this competition scores ranking, a better path is a small OOF stacker or sigmoid calibrator per strategic regime rather than global isotonic smoothing.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 specialists or use existing diverse models, collect out-of-fold predictions, then fit a simple logistic stacker or `CalibratedClassifierCV(method="sigmoid")` separately for regimes such as wet vs slick, can-finish vs cannot-finish, or early vs late race. Use only OOF base predictions for calibrator fitting.
expected_effect: Often converts similar-CV models into a better LB submission by fixing regime-specific mis-scaling without damaging ranking.
risk: Too many regimes or isotonic calibration can overfit; calibrating on in-fold predictions is pure leakage.
